name: FORUM Credit Union
description_short: FORUM Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards with nationwide ATM access through CO-OP and Alliance One networks.
description_long:
FORUM Credit Union operates as a full-service credit union providing comprehensive financial products to personal and business members. The organization maintains physical branch locations, including the Monument Circle branch in Indianapolis, alongside digital banking channels including online platforms and mobile applications. As a credit union, FORUM is structured as a member-owned cooperative rather than a shareholder-owned bank, which typically translates to competitive rates and member-focused policies.

The credit union offers a broad spectrum of financial services across savings, spending, borrowing, and planning categories. Savings products include personal savings accounts, youth and student accounts, and business savings options. Spending products feature personal checking, student checking, business checking, and credit cards. Borrowing services include personal loans, auto loans, youth loans, mortgages, home equity lines, business loans, and credit card products. Members also have access to financial coaching, calculators, and educational resources on topics like homebuying and home renovation.

FORUM distinguishes itself through extensive network access: members gain nationwide access to over 5,000 CO-OP shared branches and 5,000+ surcharge-free Alliance One ATMs across 42 states. The credit union emphasizes community involvement through programs like "Fridays with FORUM," which supports small businesses and local merchants. Additional differentiators include notary services at branches, free financial wellness checks, debt protection options, and a Private Client Group for higher-touch service.

As a traditional credit union with multiple service channels, FORUM serves members seeking comprehensive financial services with cooperative ownership benefits. The main consideration for prospective members is that some specialty services (like wealth management or investment products) may be more limited compared to large national banks. The credit union's emphasis on community giving and member education appeals to values-conscious consumers, though competitive rates depend on membership eligibility and specific product comparison. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Access to 5,000+ surcharge-free Alliance One ATMs across 42 states plus 5,000 CO-OP shared branches nationwide
Member-owned credit union structure typically offering competitive rates and member-focused policies
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, personal loans, auto loans, mortgages, home equity, and business services
Free financial wellness checks available at branches and free financial coaching services
Community involvement programs like "Fridays with FORUM" supporting local small businesses
Multiple access channels including in-person branches, online banking, mobile app, and business digital banking
Educational resources including homebuying guides, calculators, and financial literacy content
cons: Monument Circle location is lobby and ATM services only with no cash transactions available
Limited branch hours on Saturday (9:00am-1:00pm) and closed Sundays at listed location
No information provided about membership eligibility requirements or potential restrictions
Website content does not specify interest rates, APRs, or fee structures for comparison
Business loan and investment services may be less comprehensive than large national banks
best_for: Individuals seeking nationwide ATM access and branch network without surcharges
Small business owners needing business checking, business loans, and integrated banking services
Members interested in community-focused financial institutions with local giveback programs
Consumers who value member ownership and cooperative financial institution principles
diagnosis: FORUM Credit Union is best for individuals and small business owners seeking comprehensive financial services with the benefits of member ownership and extensive nationwide ATM/branch access through cooperative networks. The main caveat is that membership eligibility may have requirements, and specific rates and fees should be compared directly with the credit union before joining, as this information is not disclosed on the location-specific webpage.
services: Youth and student checking and savings accounts
Financial coaching and free financial wellness checks
Online banking and mobile app access
Nationwide CO-OP shared branch access (5,000+ locations)
Alliance One surcharge-free ATM network (5,000+ ATMs)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
